description: Main trade header table. One row per trade version. Stores general trade information common to all product types. Each event that modifies a trade creates a new version row — the latest version is the operative one.
documented_columns: Key fields only — not exhaustive. Columns absent from this file may exist in the table.

columns.name: M_CONTRACT
columns.description: Contract ID. Primary identifier for the trade. Remains unchanged across all versions and amendments. Use this to group all versions of the same trade.

columns.name: M_NB
columns.description: Trade number. Increments on physical versioning events (Restructure, Cancel & Reissue). Combined with M_CONTRACT, uniquely identifies a specific version of the trade.

columns.name: M_GID
columns.description: Global ID. External identifier assigned by post-trade infrastructure. May be blank until assigned. Used for cross-system trade correlation.

columns.name: M_TRN_FMLY
columns.description: Product family. Top level of the Murex product classification hierarchy (e.g. IRD, EQD, CURR, CRD, COM). See murex-trade-model.md Section 1.

columns.name: M_TRN_GRP
columns.description: Product group. Second level of the product classification hierarchy (e.g. RTRN, IRS, REPO). Combined with M_TRN_FMLY and M_TRN_TYPE forms the canonical product identifier.

columns.name: M_TRN_TYPE
columns.description: Product type. Third level of the product classification hierarchy (e.g. SWAP). May be empty for some products — this is valid.

columns.name: M_COMMENT_BS
columns.description: Buy / Sell direction. Indicates whether the booking entity is the buyer or seller of the trade. Values: B (Buy), S (Sell). Used for commodity trades and other products where direction is stored separately from Pay/Receive.

columns.name: M_INSTRUMENT
columns.description: Instrument code. References the specific instrument or security underlying the trade (e.g. bond identifier for a Bond Return Swap).

columns.name: M_PL_INSCUR
columns.description: P&L currency. The currency in which the trade's P&L is reported.

columns.name: M_PL_KEY1
columns.description: P&L key. Used for P&L aggregation and reporting segmentation.

columns.name: M_MKT_LABEL
columns.description: Market label. Identifies the pricing market or data source used for valuation of the trade.

columns.name: M_BTRADER
columns.description: Trader (Buy side). User ID of the trader on the buy side. PII — must be tokenised before any LLM processing.

columns.name: M_BPFOLIO
columns.description: Portfolio (Buy side). The Murex portfolio to which the buy side of the trade is assigned. Drives entity routing, accounting centre selection, and risk aggregation.

columns.name: M_BSTRATEGY
columns.description: Strategy (Buy side). Business strategy label on the buy side. Used for position aggregation and reporting.

columns.name: M_BENTITY
columns.description: Entity (Buy side). Legal entity on the buy side under which the trade is booked for regulatory and accounting purposes.

columns.name: M_STRADER
columns.description: Trader (Sell side). User ID of the trader on the sell side. PII — must be tokenised before any LLM processing.

columns.name: M_SPFOLIO
columns.description: Portfolio (Sell side). The Murex portfolio to which the sell side of the trade is assigned.

columns.name: M_SSTRATEGY
columns.description: Strategy (Sell side). Business strategy label on the sell side.

columns.name: M_SENTITY
columns.description: Entity (Sell side). Legal entity on the sell side.

columns.name: M_TRN_STATUS
columns.description: Trade status. Current lifecycle state of the trade. Values: LIVE (active, no market operations applied), DEAD (expired or terminated), MKT_OP (live but modified by a market operation, displayed as Amended). See murex-trade-model.md Section 2.

columns.name: M_TRN_DATE
columns.description: Trade date. The date on which the trade was booked.

columns.name: M_TRN_EXP
columns.description: Expiry date / Payment Date. The contractual maturity or payment date of the trade. For commodity trades: the payment date.

columns.name: M_SYS_DATE
columns.description: System date. The Murex system date at the time the row was written. Used to identify when the version was created.

columns.name: M_BRW_NOM1
columns.description: Nominal — Leg 1. The notional amount of the first leg. For commodity trades: the quantity.

columns.name: M_BRW_NOMU1
columns.description: Nominal currency — Leg 1. The currency of the Leg 1 notional. For commodity trades: the unit of measurement (see CM_UNIT_DBF).

columns.name: M_BRW_NOM2
columns.description: Nominal — Leg 2. The notional amount of the second leg. May differ from Leg 1 for cross-currency structures or where a haircut applies.

columns.name: M_BRW_NOMU2
columns.description: Nominal currency — Leg 2. The currency of the Leg 2 notional.

columns.name: M_BRW_RTE1
columns.description: Rate or price — Leg 1. For rate-based legs: the fixed or initial floating rate. For bond legs: the clean price at inception. For commodity trades: the commodity price.

columns.name: M_BRW_RTE2
columns.description: Rate or price — Leg 2. Same as M_BRW_RTE1 but for Leg 2.

columns.name: M_BRW_MRG1
columns.description: Margin — Leg 1. Additive spread over the index rate on Leg 1.

columns.name: M_BRW_MRG2
columns.description: Margin — Leg 2. Additive spread over the index rate on Leg 2.

columns.name: M_BRW_PR1
columns.description: Pay/Receive — Leg 1. Direction of Leg 1 from the booking entity's perspective. Typical values: P (Pay), R (Receive).

columns.name: M_BRW_PR2
columns.description: Pay/Receive — Leg 2. Direction of Leg 2. Typically opposite to M_BRW_PR1.

columns.name: M_PURGE_DATE
columns.description: Purge date. The date on which the trade was logically purged. Null for active trades. Logically purged trades are excluded from most datamart reports.

columns.name: M_PURGE_REF
columns.description: Purge reference. Reference identifier for the purge operation. Null for active trades.

columns.name: M_COUNTRPART
columns.description: Counterparty code. Identifies the external counterparty to the trade. Used for credit risk, confirmation matching, and settlement instructions.

columns.name: M_BRW_AE
columns.description: American / European flag. Applicable to options only. Indicates whether the option is American (can be exercised at any time before expiry) or European (can only be exercised at expiry). Null or irrelevant for non-option products.
